evaluation_semantics_notes.runtime_boundary_vs_aksharamd: in-process library call vs subprocess; no exact speed ratio claimed

per_asset.human_review_evidence:
3725 chars; German audit-report body extracted with hyphenation preserved as line breaks ('pro-
vision'). Section headings preserved.

4191 chars but each word is line-broken with heavy whitespace ('A Message from the President / Dear / Members, / I write in an / effort to share'). Reading order is fragmented; content is present but visibly damaged.
2394 chars; safety-warning content extracted with lettered list (a/b/c) preserved. Same quality as PyMuPDF4LLM.
Single-column news article extracted cleanly; body preserved.
6724 chars but rendered as broken table syntax with columns like '| EL | PERIÓDICO |'; Spanish accents preserved (PERIÓDICO, ESPAÑOL, SÁBADO). Reading order corrupted by table-syntax reconstruction.
7024 chars, cleaner than PyMuPDF4LLM's extraction of this asset but still shows magazine-layout interleaving. TOC-like content readable; body sections fragmented.
**0 chars extracted** — no OCR; Japanese magazine image produces empty output.
**0 chars extracted** — MarkItDown has no OCR; image-only UK Home Office letter produces empty output. Same failure mode as PyMuPDF4LLM; AksharaMD Marker delivered 2353 chars.
**0 chars extracted** — no OCR; scientific-taxonomy image plate produces empty output.

4646 chars but heavily fragmented: 'It / follows / (2015/1017). / the European Fund / This report presents the results of the' — words split across lines. Content present but ordering severely corrupted.

7434 chars but TOC entries have concatenated page numbers ('103101' = '10' + '3101'). Strikethrough markup is NOT preserved as markdown syntax (unlike PyMuPDF4LLM). Content readable but structurally damaged.
German dense-text extracted; layout not fully verified at review DPI; typical MarkItDown quality on European text.
598 chars; Lorem Ipsum body extracted with line-break at column width but content complete. Same as PyMuPDF4LLM.
Image caption text extracted; image not rendered as placeholder (MarkItDown emits fewer image tokens than PyMuPDF4LLM).
Multi-page LaTeX doc extracted with content per page preserved.
**0 chars** — single image page; no OCR.
**0 chars** — single image page; no OCR.
**53 chars** — 'Background' repeated 4 times, no other content. No OCR; content lost.
**0 chars** — single image page; no OCR.
6 chars — essentially empty output.
24 chars — only form labels 'Name / Check / Submit / 1'. Static text lost.
120 chars — sparse content; form structure lost.
22 chars: 'حَبيبي habibi حَبيبي'. Arabic script preserved but reading order not verified; near-empty output. Similar degradation to PyMuPDF4LLM.

2521 chars; extracts datasheet content ('Rotary Potentiometer', 'Electrocomponents Industrial'). **MarkItDown parses this file where PyMuPDF4LLM raises IndexError.** Content structured as table but readable. Supplementary review — outside primary matched set.
Caption text extracted; image reference stripped.
**0 chars** — no OCR.
**0 chars** — no OCR.

13201 chars but rendered as broken markdown table syntax ('| Two-Column | | | | Document | with | Lorem'). Content present but not readable as normal prose; table reconstruction misfires on the two-column layout.
Multi-page image references PDF; small text extracted, most content lost.
